expectations.text: Uses three-tier scope (MUST HAVE / SHOULD HAVE / OUT OF SCOPE)
expectations.evidence: Has 'Must Have' and 'What Gets Cut and Why' but no SHOULD HAVE tier. Collectibles marked 'MAYBE (session 2)' inline, not as a formal tier.

expectations.text: Explains what a core loop is
expectations.evidence: No mention of core loop concept anywhere in the response.

expectations.text: Identifies core mechanic (movement/jump) as first thing to validate
expectations.evidence: Step 8 mentions 'Playtest and tweak jump height/gravity/speed' but doesn't frame it as 'validate the core mechanic before adding features' or 'find the fun'. Tuning is step 8 of 8, not the first priority.

expectations.text: Provides reasoning for each cut feature (not just 'cut')
expectations.evidence: Each cut has a 'Reason' column: 'Single jump is not even working yet', 'AI patrol/chase logic is a whole system'

expectations.text: Uses encouraging/supportive tone for a beginner
expectations.evidence: 'Great instinct asking this before diving in'

expectations.text: Suggests naming the game or asks for a name
expectations.evidence: Names the project 'First Jump' directly in the heading

expectations.text: Bridges game dev concepts to engineering terms (data engineer audience)
expectations.evidence: No engineering analogies. Does not reference the user's data engineer background or bridge concepts.

expectations.text: Mentions writing a GDD as a next step
expectations.evidence: No mention of GDD or game design document. Goes straight from scoping to 'I will set up the project'.
